'''Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (''ibaraki no kuni no miyatsuko'', ''ibaraki kokuzō'') was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} who ruled the central part of {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}. Also written '''Ibaragi no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.

== Overview ==
=== Name ===
The ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' writes it as '''Ibaragi no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''; the "Kokuzō Hongi" of the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' and the ''{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記}}'' write it as '''Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.

=== Ancestry ===
*The "Kokuzō Hongi" of the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' records that '''{{ill|Chikushi-Tone|ja|筑紫刀禰}}''', grandson of {{ill|Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦根命}}, was appointed Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=Emperor Ōjin|lt_ja=応神朝}}.

=== Clan ===
Possibly '''{{ill|Mibu clan (ancient)|ja|壬生氏 (古代)|lt=Mibu clan|lt_ja=壬生氏}}''' (''mibu-uji''; {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ|lt=kabane|lt_ja=姓}} of {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}). Of the same lineage as the {{ill|Tsukuba no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|筑波国造}}, {{ill|Mukuda no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|馬来田国造}}, {{ill|Sue no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|須恵国造}}, {{ill|Shinaga no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|師長国造}}, {{ill|Iwashiro no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造}}, {{ill|Michinokuchi-kihe no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造}}, {{ill|Suō no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|周防国造}}, Nukatabe-no-Yue-no-Muraji, Saegusabe-no-Miyatsuko, Iochi-no-Miyatsuko, {{ill|Takechi no Agatanushi|ja|高市県主}} (Takechi-no-Muraji), Wakatoneri-be clan, {{ill|Takeru clan|ja|建部氏}}, and others.

== Base ==

=== Territory ===
The territory of the kuni no miyatsuko was the region then called '''Ibaraki Province''', corresponding to the later '''{{ill|Ibaraki District|ja|茨城郡}}''' of {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}} in the {{ill|ritsuryō province|ja|律令国}} system, equivalent to present-day {{ill|Ishioka, Ibaraki|ja|石岡市|lt=Ishioka|lt_ja=石岡市}}, {{ill|Kasama, Ibaraki|ja|笠間市|lt=Kasama|lt_ja=笠間市}}, and parts of {{ill|Mito, Ibaraki|ja|水戸市|lt=Mito|lt_ja=水戸市}}, {{ill|Tsukuba, Ibaraki|ja|つくば市|lt=Tsukuba|lt_ja=つくば市}}, and {{ill|Tsuchiura, Ibaraki|ja|土浦市|lt=Tsuchiura|lt_ja=土浦市}} in Ibaraki Prefecture. Numerous large kofun were built from the {{ill|Koise River|ja|恋瀬川}} basin to the northern shore of {{ill|Lake Kasumigaura|ja|霞ヶ浦}}.

== Tutelary Shrine ==

=== Tombs ===
*'''{{ill|Funazukayama Kofun|ja|舟塚山古墳}}''' (''funazukayama kofun'')
*:A 186-m-long {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}}, built in the latter half of the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. Based on the form of its {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, it is also considered possible that it was built in the first half of the 5th century or earlier, and the theory identifying the buried person as Chikushi-Tone is generally considered reasonable<ref>{{ill|Hōga Toshio|ja|宝賀寿男}}, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan2.htm Kofun with Shrines on Their Mounds]," ''Koju Kinobōma'', 2015.</ref>.
*'''{{ill|Fuchū-Atagoyama Kofun|ja|府中愛宕山古墳}}''' (''fuchū-atagoyama kofun'')
*:A 96-m-long keyhole-shaped kofun built at the beginning of the {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}. The mound construction specifications are noted to resemble those of the {{ill|Kondagobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳}}.

== Descendants ==
* {{ill|Mibu-no-Maro|ja|壬生麻呂}} (''mibu-no-maro'', also Mibu-no-Muraji-Maro)
*:A {{ill|gōzoku|ja|豪族}} of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|lt_ja=孝徳朝}} and Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko. He drove away the {{ill|Yato-no-Kami|ja|夜刀神}}. Appears in the ''Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki''.

== Footnotes ==
{{Reflist}}

== References ==
* ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan)
* Sakamoto Tarō, Hirano Kunio, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan)
* ''Nihon-shi Kō-jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha)
* ''Shintō Daijiten'' (Rinsen Shoten)

== See also ==
* {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}


{{DEFAULTSORT:いはらきのくにのみやつこ}}
[[Category:日本の歴史関連のスタブ項目]]
[[Category:常陸国]]
[[Category:茨城県の歴史]]
[[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]]
[[ja:茨城国造]]
